finding_results.evidence: Revised 4 of the 8 flagged instances plus 1 unflagged instance. Removed: chapters/chapter_11.md:58 (rewritten as em-dash positive assertion: 'the pictures ran out and he wanted to know what happened next'), chapters/chapter_11.md:102 (split into period + positive assertion: 'His hands needed something to hold.'), chapters/chapter_11.md:162 (rewritten as positive action: 'Mira eased the laptop closed'), chapters/chapter_11.md:214 (rewritten as direct description: 'air she'd been holding, finally let go'). Also revised unflagged instance at chapters/chapter_11.md:188 ('not prayer, just' rewritten as 'holding her own hands because she couldn't hold anything else'). Retained 4 from original 8: chapters/chapter_11.md:3 (opening voice), chapters/chapter_11.md:164 ('not the flat passivity'), chapters/chapter_11.md:200 ('not thrown, just released'), chapters/chapter_11.md:202 (emotional pivot: 'Not forgiven, not fixed. Just beside the point.').
finding_results.notes: 5 instances revised, 4 from the packet's 8 retained. Count meets acceptance target of ≤4 from the identified set. Additional unflagged instances (lines 80, 96, 100, 126) serve different syntactic functions (manner description, causal explanation, simile modifier) and were not targeted.

finding_results.evidence: Revised 2 of 3 instances. chapters/chapter_11.md:158 — replaced 'the way people looked at screens when they'd stopped reading' with Dan-specific observation: 'Her eyes had stopped moving across the lines. She was looking through the screen at whatever the words had built behind it.' chapters/chapter_11.md:210 — replaced 'She said the phrase the way she'd say the name of something diagnosed in a person she loved' with direct rendering: 'She handled the phrase like a diagnosis — carefully, as if pronouncing it right could make it manageable.' Retained 1 instance: chapters/chapter_11.md:13 ('at the angle people held phones when they were processing bad news').
finding_results.notes: The retained instance at line 13 is the chapter's strongest use of the construction — the phone-angle observation is physically specific and earns its generalization as a POV detail. The revised instances now use character-specific description rather than the universalizing formula.

finding_results.evidence: Revised 4 instances in narrator prose. chapters/chapter_11.md:11 — dropped 'particular' ('the rhythm of Mira walking toward a problem'). chapters/chapter_11.md:25 — dropped 'particular' ('the staleness of a man who hadn't figured out'). chapters/chapter_11.md:164 — replaced 'particular' with 'held' ('His held stillness'). chapters/chapter_11.md:184 — dropped 'particular' ('the temperature of a third-floor walk-up'). Retained 1 instance: chapters/chapter_11.md:192 ('the particular gray of a New York apartment in the hour before anyone turns on a lamp'). Note: chapters/chapter_11.md:56 contains 'particular' in the italicized AI assessment text, which is institutional register, not narrator voice.
finding_results.notes: 1 instance remains in narrator prose (line 192), well under the ≤2 target. The retained instance is the chapter's most evocative use — the elaborating clause ('in the hour before anyone turns on a lamp') works in concert with the adjective rather than replacing it. All removed instances had elaborating clauses that already performed the specifying work.

finding_results.evidence: Reduced from 6 to 4 instances with varied constructions. Retained: chapters/chapter_11.md:29 (establishing sound: 'the faint rhythmic tap of pencil on paper'), chapters/chapter_11.md:158 (mid-chapter: 'The pencil scratched in Theo's room'). Removed: former line 184 instance ('the pencil resumed its small scratching') and former line 192 instance ('The pencil scratched'). Added: chapters/chapter_11.md:212 (after bathroom trip: 'the scratch of pencil on paper resumed behind it' — arrives after 54 lines of silence from the motif, carrying fresh weight). Revised closing: chapters/chapter_11.md:240 ('pencil on paper, small and steady' — varied from all previous constructions). Also revised chapters/chapter_11.md:166 for sensory consistency: changed 'The pencil-tapping' to 'The pencil in Theo's room had gone quiet' to resolve tap/scratch inconsistency and create a more evocative silence beat.
finding_results.notes: 4 pencil-sound instances remain. 'The pencil scratched' exact construction appears once (line 158), under the ≤2 limit. The four constructions are now distinct: 'tap' (29), 'scratched' (158), 'scratch...resumed' (212), 'pencil on paper' (240). The motif's final instances arrive after Theo's bathroom trip, where his physical movement through the apartment refreshes the reader's awareness of the sound.

summary: All four findings resolved. CH11-PROSE-01: negation-correction pattern reduced from 8 to 4 identified instances (plus 1 unflagged instance also revised). CH11-PROSE-02: universalizing construction reduced from 3 to 1 instance, with replacements using character-specific observation. CH11-PROSE-03: 'particular' reduced from 5 narrator-prose instances to 1, keeping the most evocative usage. CH11-PROSE-04: pencil motif reduced from 6 to 4 instances with varied constructions and improved spacing; the motif's resumed appearance after Theo's bathroom trip carries fresh weight. Additional copyedit work: fixed sensory inconsistency (tap/scratch) at line 166, revised Mira's line at 200 to avoid verbatim echo of style-bible example line, fixed elliptical clipping in line 214 ('more than strategy' revised to 'something past anger, past strategy'). No continuity contradictions introduced; all ages, dates, object states, and spatial details verified against continuity sheet. Heading contract preserved. No sanitization: the chapter's emotional rawness (the machine's clinical language applied to a child, the parents' shame, the 'fucking motif persistence' profanity) is maintained at full specificity.
